mapping.a_element: zero-point (vacuum) fluctuations of the quantum field underlying the noise/energy
mapping.b_element: zero-point vacuum fluctuations underlying the Casimir energy

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the shared physical substrate — zero-point/vacuum fluctuations of the quantum field — which is a common topic, not shared machinery. Paper A's method is noise-spectral-density measurement in Josephson junctions plus an energy-density cutoff argument bounding vacuum energy against cosmological dark energy (and yielding 1/f noise), whereas Paper B's method is the scattering / transition-(T-)operator formalism that computes Casimir forces from scattering data; these are different governing processes with no role-for-role mapping (no cutoff/noise-spectrum analogue in B, no scattering/T-operator analogue in A).
